title: [InstCombine] Fold "extract (select (cond, insert(agg, elem), FV))"
summary: The developers generalized an optimization by recognizing that `extractvalue(select(cond, insertvalue(...), ...))` can be simplified by hoisting the extraction through the select. Instead of hardcoding for a specific aggregate type or number of insertions, they used `simplifyExtractValueInst` to generically extract from both select arms. If simplification succeeds (e.g., extracting from an `insertvalue` yields the inserted element), they reuse the result; otherwise, they create a new explicit `extractvalue`. This makes the transformation type-agnostic and handles any aggregate structure. The key insight is to separate pattern matching from the core logic: match the `extractvalue(select)` pattern, then delegate to existing simplification routines for the actual extraction, ensuring correctness and reusability across all aggregate types.
